epic_name: Synthetic Frontier sFX Derivatives Market -- Technical Design Brief (v2)
summary: Decomposed the technical design brief into granular MVP-ready tasks covering index data, trading core, risk/vault controls, stress management, transparency/governance, and launch validation.
warnings: The named epic decomposition skill was not available in the session skill list, so the decomposition follows the provided contract directly.
The epic is architecture-level and under-specifies implementation stack, storage, and deployment choices; tasks infer conservative deliverables needed for an MVP.
UI viewability requirements were applied only to the public transparency dashboard task because it is the only clearly user-facing screen in the brief.
Dependency ordering is inferred from system prerequisites and should be rechecked if the implementation stack introduces additional constraints.

tasks.title: Define macro volatility index schema and source contract
tasks.workstream_goal: Produce a deterministic, publishable index foundation for all listed instruments.
tasks.purpose: Establish the canonical schema, source taxonomy, and field definitions for each macro volatility index so downstream pricing, funding, liquidation, and transparency modules operate on consistent data.
tasks.input: Technical brief sections 2, 5, 6, and 9; target phase-1 instruments (NGN, KES, GHS, ZAR); available reference-rate and proxy data assumptions.
tasks.output: Versioned index schema specification, source classification matrix, and per-instrument field dictionary.
tasks.action: Write the canonical data contract for index inputs and outputs, including required fields, nullable rules, precision, freshness expectations, and publication semantics for each instrument.
verification: Schema document defines all required index input and output fields with types and units.
At least one worked example is provided for a phase-1 instrument showing raw inputs and resulting normalized records.
Field definitions explicitly support funding, liquidation reference, and circuit-breaker anchoring use cases.

tasks.title: Specify source ingestion and normalization pipeline
tasks.workstream_goal: Produce a deterministic, publishable index foundation for all listed instruments.
tasks.purpose: Translate heterogeneous official, offshore, and proxy data sources into a normalized stream suitable for index computation and health checks.
tasks.input: A1 schema; anticipated upstream source formats and delivery cadence; epic requirements for medianisation, smoothing, and source observability.
tasks.output: Ingestion pipeline design with adapters, normalization rules, fallback handling, and freshness/error policies.
tasks.action: Define ingestion stages, transformation rules, quality checks, and fallback behavior for all supported source categories.
verification: Pipeline specification maps every source category to a normalization rule and freshness threshold.
Failure modes for missing, stale, or conflicting source data are documented with deterministic handling.
Normalization output matches the schema established in A1.

tasks.title: Design index calculation, weighting, and smoothing engine
tasks.workstream_goal: Produce a deterministic, publishable index foundation for all listed instruments.
tasks.purpose: Define the exact algorithm that converts normalized source observations into the macro volatility index referenced by market controls.
tasks.input: A1 schema; A2 ingestion specification; epic methodology for volatility weighting, smoothing, and medianisation.
tasks.output: Deterministic index calculation specification, pseudocode, and test vectors for phase-1 instruments.
tasks.action: Specify the calculation sequence, weighting logic, smoothing windows, and confidence output, including reference examples and edge-case behavior.
verification: Calculation steps are deterministic and reproducible from normalized source inputs.
Test vectors cover at least normal, source-conflict, and degraded-data scenarios.
Outputs are explicitly consumable by funding, liquidation, and circuit-breaker modules.

tasks.title: Define oracle health and index divergence monitoring rules
tasks.workstream_goal: Produce a deterministic, publishable index foundation for all listed instruments.
tasks.purpose: Create the monitoring logic that detects source instability and index divergence conditions before they propagate into trading and risk decisions.
tasks.input: A2 ingestion specification; A3 calculation engine; epic stress and circuit-breaker triggers.
tasks.output: Oracle health scoring rules, divergence thresholds, and alert/event contract for downstream controls.
tasks.action: Define source quorum requirements, instability heuristics, divergence measurements versus market price, and emitted events for automated responses.
verification: Health and divergence rules produce explicit event outputs consumable by stress and circuit-breaker workflows.
Thresholds cover source instability and index divergence conditions called out in the epic.
Monitoring rules distinguish degraded mode from hard-stop conditions.

tasks.title: Define perpetual instrument and market configuration model
tasks.workstream_goal: Specify the tradable instrument, price formation, and funding mechanics for the MVP venue.
tasks.purpose: Represent each perpetual market with a complete configuration model that captures margining, pricing references, listing parameters, and isolation constraints.
tasks.input: Epic sections 2, 3, 8, and 9; phase-1 instrument list; A1-A3 index artifacts.
tasks.output: Instrument configuration schema and listing template for perpetual contracts.
tasks.action: Define the per-market config required to list and operate each synthetic macro perpetual without cross-margin or token balances.
verification: Configuration model captures stablecoin margining, isolated exposure, and index reference settings.
Listing template is sufficient to instantiate all planned phase-1 instruments.
Required fields for trading engine, risk engine, and transparency publication are identified.

tasks.title: Specify central limit order book and price formation rules
tasks.workstream_goal: Specify the tradable instrument, price formation, and funding mechanics for the MVP venue.
tasks.purpose: Define how orders match, how price is formed by order flow, and how market-state outputs feed depth, imbalance, and stress metrics.
tasks.input: B1 instrument config; epic sections 2 and 3; risk requirements that rely on order-book depth visibility.
tasks.output: CLOB behavior specification covering order lifecycle, matching priority, market state outputs, and depth reporting.
tasks.action: Document the order lifecycle, matching rules, depth visibility requirements, and derived order-book metrics consumed by risk and stress modules.
verification: Spec states clearly that trading price is order-flow determined rather than mechanically pegged to the index.
Outputs required for depth, imbalance, and thinning-rate calculations are explicitly enumerated.
Matching and market-state rules are internally consistent for phase-1 low-leverage launch.

tasks.title: Design funding rate calculation and settlement model
tasks.workstream_goal: Specify the tradable instrument, price formation, and funding mechanics for the MVP venue.
tasks.purpose: Turn the conceptual funding formula into an implementable and transparent rule set tied to imbalance, volatility, and open-interest velocity.
tasks.input: A3 index outputs; B1 instrument config; B2 market-state outputs; epic section 4.2.
tasks.output: Funding formula specification, parameter definitions, settlement cadence, and disclosure-ready examples.
tasks.action: Define the funding formula, update cadence, sign convention, settlement path, and disclosure examples for normal and stressed conditions.
verification: Funding design uses continuous scaling with no cliff thresholds.
Worked examples show amplification under imbalance and stressed volatility conditions.
Funding outputs are publishable in the transparency layer without revealing proprietary code.

tasks.title: Define vault accounting and exposure cap model
tasks.workstream_goal: Model the DAO backstop, leverage controls, and liquidation protections required to survive volatility shocks.
tasks.purpose: Specify how the DAO vault absorbs imbalances, earns fees, tracks per-instrument exposure, and enforces capital protection limits.
tasks.input: B1 instrument config; B2 market-state outputs; epic sections 3, 4, 6, and 8.
tasks.output: Vault ledger model, exposure-cap rules, and per-instrument isolation specification.
tasks.action: Define the accounting model and control rules for isolated vault exposure, fee accrual, funding participation, and cap enforcement.
verification: Model preserves instrument isolation and prevents cross-margin contagion.
Exposure-cap logic is explicit for imbalance absorption, liquidation counterparty behavior, and backstop limits.
Ledger outputs support transparency publication of vault capital and exposure state.

tasks.title: Design dynamic leverage band engine
tasks.workstream_goal: Model the DAO backstop, leverage controls, and liquidation protections required to survive volatility shocks.
tasks.purpose: Define how leverage compresses or expands based on volatility, imbalance, depth, and stress velocity within governance-approved bands.
tasks.input: A3 index metrics; B2 order-book metrics; C1 vault exposure model; epic section 4.1.
tasks.output: Leverage-band algorithm specification with inputs, bounds, and recalculation triggers.
tasks.action: Specify the leverage adjustment function, hard bounds, recalculation cadence, and publishing behavior for effective leverage bands.
verification: Engine respects initial 1x-2x launch posture and configurable maximum leverage caps.
Adjustment inputs include all four factors named in the epic.
Examples demonstrate compression during thin liquidity or high-stress conditions.

tasks.title: Design spread elasticity and quoting protection rules
tasks.workstream_goal: Model the DAO backstop, leverage controls, and liquidation protections required to survive volatility shocks.
tasks.purpose: Define the dynamic minimum spread control that widens during turbulence to protect liquidity and the vault from toxic flow.
tasks.input: B2 market-state outputs; C1 vault exposure model; epic section 4.3 and stress metrics in section 5.1.
tasks.output: Spread elasticity formula and control-state specification.
tasks.action: Specify spread widening logic, recalculation cadence, and control outputs used by market-making and market-state publication.
verification: Formula explicitly uses the four drivers named in the epic.
Control outputs can be consumed by both trading safeguards and public transparency feeds.
Scenarios cover calm, elevated, and shock conditions.

tasks.title: Specify liquidation workflow and vault backstop intervention
tasks.workstream_goal: Model the DAO backstop, leverage controls, and liquidation protections required to survive volatility shocks.
tasks.purpose: Define how positions are liquidated, how the vault acts as counterparty, and how penalties and residual risk are handled without discretionary intervention.
tasks.input: A3 index reference outputs; B1 instrument config; C1 vault model; C2 leverage bands.
tasks.output: Liquidation state machine, penalty model, and vault intervention rules.
tasks.action: Document liquidation triggers, reference prices, execution sequence, vault takeover logic, and post-liquidation accounting.
verification: Workflow uses the index only as liquidation reference, consistent with the epic.
Vault intervention and penalty handling are deterministic and non-discretionary.
Design identifies safeguards for clustered liquidations during stress.

tasks.title: Define stress detection metrics and event thresholds
tasks.workstream_goal: Detect instability early and apply rule-based market protections without manual intervention.
tasks.purpose: Operationalize the stress metrics named in the brief so market controls can react to deteriorating conditions consistently.
tasks.input: A4 oracle health events; B2 order-book metrics; C1-C3 risk control outputs; epic section 5.1.
tasks.output: Stress metric definitions, threshold bands, severity levels, and event payload schema.
tasks.action: Define exact calculations, lookback windows, and severity thresholds for each stress metric and the event contract they emit.
verification: All four stress metrics from the epic have explicit definitions and thresholds.
Event schema is sufficient for automated response orchestration and audit logging.
Thresholds distinguish warning, elevated, and emergency states.

tasks.title: Design automated response orchestrator for stress events
tasks.workstream_goal: Detect instability early and apply rule-based market protections without manual intervention.
tasks.purpose: Map stress events to automatic leverage compression, funding multiplier changes, spread widening, position-cap tightening, and open-interest enforcement.
tasks.input: D1 stress events; C2 leverage engine; C3 spread control; B3 funding model; C1 vault caps; epic section 5.2.
tasks.output: Response orchestration matrix and state transition rules for automated controls.
tasks.action: Define the deterministic control actions triggered by each stress state, including ordering, cooldowns, and conflict resolution.
verification: Each automatic response listed in the epic is mapped to explicit trigger conditions.
Orchestration defines deterministic precedence when multiple stress signals fire together.
Response changes are publishable for audit and transparency review.

tasks.title: Design circuit breaker state machine and staged reopening rules
tasks.workstream_goal: Detect instability early and apply rule-based market protections without manual intervention.
tasks.purpose: Specify when markets halt and how they reopen gradually when divergence, source instability, or depth collapse conditions are met.
tasks.input: A4 oracle/divergence monitoring; B2 depth metrics; D1 stress thresholds; D2 automated response design; epic section 5.3.
tasks.output: Circuit-breaker state machine, trigger thresholds, reopening schedule, and operator-visible status model.
tasks.action: Define halt triggers, allowed transitions, staged reopening checks, and status outputs for each instrument.
verification: Triggers cover index divergence, data source instability, and order-book depth collapse.
Reopening logic is gradual, rule-based, and requires explicit recovery conditions.
State machine prevents discretionary manual intervention outside predefined emergency logic.

tasks.title: Define public transparency data contract and disclosure pack
tasks.workstream_goal: Expose live market state and governance controls in a deterministic, reviewable form.
tasks.purpose: Create the exact public-facing data contract and formula disclosures required to make system state and rule execution transparent.
tasks.input: A3 index outputs; B3 funding model; C1-C3 risk controls; D2-D3 control states; epic section 6.
tasks.output: Transparency schema, disclosure copy, and publication field catalog.
tasks.action: Define every public metric, its source system, update cadence, disclosure wording, and any redaction boundaries between formulas and proprietary implementation.
verification: Contract covers all live system state fields listed in the epic.
Formula disclosures are understandable without exposing proprietary code internals.
Each field maps to an upstream owner and update cadence.

tasks.title: Specify governance parameter band and emergency override framework
tasks.workstream_goal: Expose live market state and governance controls in a deterministic, reviewable form.
tasks.purpose: Define what governance can change, what hard limits constrain it, and how emergency overrides operate without introducing discretionary market intervention.
tasks.input: C1-C3 control models; D3 circuit-breaker design; epic section 7.
tasks.output: Governance control matrix, parameter-band registry, and emergency override policy.
tasks.action: Document governance-adjustable parameters, immutable bounds, approval paths, and the predeclared emergency procedures allowed in production.
verification: All governance controls named in the epic are captured with bounded ranges.
Emergency override logic is predefined and auditable rather than discretionary.
Policy distinguishes routine parameter changes from emergency actions.

tasks.title: Build public market-state transparency dashboard MVP
tasks.workstream_goal: Expose live market state and governance controls in a deterministic, reviewable form.
tasks.purpose: Deliver a user-facing dashboard that displays public vault, imbalance, open-interest, leverage, funding, volatility, and risk-band data for MVP review and operator confidence.
tasks.input: E1 transparency schema; D3 market status model; selected implementation stack and local runtime assumptions.
tasks.output: Runnable local dashboard with data-binding to transparency feeds, access script, and verification evidence including screenshots.
tasks.action: Implement the dashboard, wire it to the transparency contract, add a one-step local start script, and capture verification evidence for the working UI.
verification: A root-level or relevant starter script is provided that launches the UI and prints the localhost URL.
The exact local URL to open is documented and reachable after startup.
A startup smoke test confirms the UI boots without immediate dependency or configuration crashes.
Screenshot evidence is captured in the verification output showing the dashboard in a working state.
If backend data sources are unavailable, the UI starts in a resilient fallback or mock-data mode rather than crashing.

tasks.title: Define isolated margin and per-instrument containment model
tasks.workstream_goal: Prepare an MVP launch package that is conservative, testable, and resilient under shock conditions.
tasks.purpose: Translate the no-cross-margin design principle into an explicit containment model that constrains contagion and simplifies launch risk.
tasks.input: B1 instrument configs; C1 vault model; epic section 8.
tasks.output: Isolation model specification covering collateral scope, vault boundaries, and failure containment assumptions.
tasks.action: Document how isolated margin, vault accounting, and failure domains are enforced per instrument and what shared services remain outside the containment boundary.
verification: Isolation rules prevent cross-instrument margining and vault leakage.
Failure scenarios show how losses remain contained to a single instrument domain.
Shared dependencies that could still create systemic risk are explicitly listed.

tasks.title: Prepare phase-1 instrument listing and cap configuration pack
tasks.workstream_goal: Prepare an MVP launch package that is conservative, testable, and resilient under shock conditions.
tasks.purpose: Assemble the initial launch configuration for 3-4 instruments with conservative leverage and exposure settings.
tasks.input: A1-A3 index artifacts; B1 config model; C1-C2 risk controls; epic section 9.
tasks.output: Phase-1 listing pack with per-instrument parameters, leverage bands, exposure caps, and launch assumptions.
tasks.action: Create the launch-ready configuration set for NGN, KES, GHS, and ZAR (or confirmed subset) using conservative startup parameters.
verification: Listing pack covers all selected phase-1 instruments with complete required fields.
Leverage and exposure settings reflect the brief's conservative launch posture.
Configuration references valid upstream index and risk-control artifacts.

tasks.title: Build shock simulation and scenario validation harness
tasks.workstream_goal: Prepare an MVP launch package that is conservative, testable, and resilient under shock conditions.
tasks.purpose: Create a repeatable scenario harness that tests the venue against the success criterion of surviving a 30-50% macro shock with functioning liquidity and intact vault capital.
tasks.input: B2-B3 trading rules; C1-C4 risk/vault controls; D1-D3 stress logic; F2 phase-1 configs; epic section 10.
tasks.output: Scenario library, simulation harness specification, and scorecard template for shock-event validation.
tasks.action: Define and implement the scenario set, evaluation metrics, and pass/fail scorecard used to validate survivability under macro shock conditions.
verification: Harness includes at least one 30% shock and one 50% shock scenario.
Scorecard evaluates vault capital integrity, liquidity continuity, funding stabilization, transparency outputs, and governance stability.
Scenario outputs are reproducible and attributable to specific control reactions.

tasks.title: Produce MVP launch runbook and readiness decision package
tasks.workstream_goal: Prepare an MVP launch package that is conservative, testable, and resilient under shock conditions.
tasks.purpose: Consolidate the design, configs, scenarios, and operator checks into a single decision-ready package for MVP launch approval.
tasks.input: E1-E3 transparency deliverables; E2 governance policy; F1-F3 isolation, config, and simulation outputs.
tasks.output: Launch runbook, readiness checklist, dependency register, and go/no-go decision memo.
tasks.action: Assemble the operational procedures, readiness checklist, evidence links, and unresolved-risk register required for a controlled MVP release.
verification: Runbook includes startup, monitoring, halt, reopening, and incident-escalation procedures.
Readiness package links to configuration, transparency, governance, and shock-test evidence.
Open risks and assumptions are explicitly listed with owners or follow-up actions.
